summary.intent: Merge main into the reqwest-0.13 upgrade branch, then address PR review comments about missing features and lockfile regeneration.
summary.outcome: Merged main, resolved Cargo.toml conflict keeping reqwest 0.13 with all features, added the `query` feature, regenerated Cargo.lock, verified the build passes, and pushed.

repo: The workspace is a Cargo monorepo with a shared Cargo.toml; reqwest is declared there as a workspace dependency.
reqwest 0.13 makes the `query` feature opt-in (it was implicit in 0.12), requiring an explicit feature flag.
Third-party crates (async-openai, chromiumoxide, matrix-sdk, oauth2, reqwest-eventsource) still pull in reqwest 0.12, so both versions coexist in Cargo.lock — this is expected and not a bug.
PR comments are generated by a Greptile bot that performs static analysis on the diff.

code.finding: reqwest workspace dependency was upgraded from 0.12 to 0.13.2 with features: json, multipart, socks, stream, form, query. The socks feature came from main; form and the version bump came from the branch.

workflow: After a version bump in Cargo.toml, `cargo update` alone may not switch workspace crates — explicitly checking `cargo.lock` grep output is necessary to confirm resolution.
Running a full workspace build (`cargo build`) after lockfile regeneration is the right smoke-test before pushing dependency changes.

friction: Merge conflict in Cargo.toml required manual inspection to determine which features came from each side.
`cargo update` did not immediately produce the expected lockfile change, requiring multiple verification steps to confirm workspace crates resolved to 0.13.
open_items: PR description should be updated to note the presence of three reqwest versions (0.12.x from third-party crates, 0.13.x for workspace crates) to address the Greptile informational comment.
Third-party crates (async-openai, chromiumoxide, matrix-sdk, oauth2, reqwest-eventsource) still depend on reqwest 0.12 — upgrading them is deferred work.
